prompt: After hernia surgery with temporary lifting restrictions, how can I minimize muscle loss and when should I return to training?
expected_output: They said nutrition alone is usually weak for preventing detraining losses compared with actual loading. Eric noted that studies using very high leucine intakes (about 15-19 g/day extra) sometimes show partial benefit, while lower doses (around 10 g/day or less extra) are often not very effective. Even then, he emphasized that safe tissue loading is still the key if medically allowed. For return-to-training timing, they refused to give a universal timeline and said to work closely with your surgeon/clinical team, even if that means asking very specific follow-up questions. They also stressed that regaining lost strength is typically faster than building it the first time, so being conservative short-term is usually the better long-term play.
assertions: Answer captures the core SBS position on post-surgery detraining and return
Answer includes key specific details, numbers, or mechanisms mentioned by the hosts
Answer acknowledges important caveats or limitations discussed by the hosts
Answer does not contradict the hosts' stated position
